search_parameters.q: how to clean a dishwasher filter

search_information.query_displayed: how to clean a dishwasher filter

text_blocks.answer: To clean your dishwasher filter, pull out the bottom rack, unlock and remove the filter assembly from the bottom of the tub, and rinse it under warm water. Soak stubborn grime or hard water buildup in hot, soapy water with a splash of white vinegar. Gently scrub with a soft brush, then reassemble.
For a deeper look at the process, here are step-by-step instructions:
1. Locate and Remove the Filter
2. Wash the Filter
Rinse: Rinse the filter under the warmest running water possible to clear out large food particles.
3. Clean the Drain

While the filter is out, take a moment to look into the drain opening at the bottom of the dishwasher. Scoop out any loose food bits, glass, or debris sitting in the sump area to prevent future clogs.
4. Reinstall the Filter

Put the filter assembly back together, place it back into the bottom of the dishwasher tub, and twist it clockwise until you feel or hear it lock into place. It should feel secure and not turn freely.

Tip: Experts on platforms like Home Depot generally recommend checking and cleaning your dishwasher filter at least once a month to ensure your dishes stay spotless and your machine operates efficiently. You can also refer to your exact dishwasher manual (such as those from Whirlpool Product Help or GE Appliances) for model-specific removal and locking instructions.

related_questions.question: How to tell if a dishwasher filter is clogged?
related_questions.answer: Signs your dishwasher filter needs cleaningSoil or other food particles are visible.Cleaning performance has worsened (e.g., there are food remnants on your dishes after a cycle).Dishes are gritty or grimy to the touch after they've been cleaned.
related_questions.answer_highlight: Signs your dishwasher filter needs cleaning

answer_list.text: Soil or other food particles are visible.
Cleaning performance has worsened (e.g., there are food remnants on your dishes after a cycle).
Dishes are gritty or grimy to the touch after they've been cleaned.

source.title: How to Clean a Dishwasher Filter in 3 Steps - Whirlpool
